title: rubus carpinifolius subsp. montanus
intro: Rubus carpinifolius subsp. montanus, also known as the Mountain Raspberry or Mountain Black Raspberry, is a species of raspberry native to mountainous regions in East Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, which have been found to exhibit antioxidant and antiviral activities. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, such as supporting cardiovascular health and immune function, and has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and fever. Rubus carpinifolius subsp. montanus is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and can also be consumed fresh as a fruit, making it a versatile and accessible herbal remedy.
